title: Fable 1M window in statusline
goal: Extend windowForModel() in src/statusline/statusline.ts so the Fable model gets a 1M context window: detect 'fable' in the model name and, as a future-proof fallback, the '[1m]' suffix in the model id; cover both paths plus the unchanged 200k default (e.g. Haiku) with tests in statusline.test.ts.

steps.title: Fable detection in windowForModel
steps.detail: windowForModel() in src/statusline/statusline.ts returns 1M when the model name contains 'fable' (same pattern as sonnet/opus); JSDoc comment updated to mention Fable.

steps.title: Read model.id and detect [1m] suffix
steps.detail: StatusInput gains model.id; buildData passes it to windowForModel as an optional second parameter; an id containing '[1m]' returns 1M regardless of the display name — future-proof fallback for new long-context models.

steps.title: Tests: Fable, [1m] suffix, 200k default
steps.detail: statusline.test.ts covers: 'Fable 5' → 1M; unknown name with '[1m]' id → 1M; 'Haiku 4.5' and empty input stay 200k; buildData reads model.id from the status JSON.

steps.title: Green: npm test + npm run build
steps.detail: Full test suite and typecheck/build pass without errors.

resolvedVerify: Statusline shows 1M for a real Fable session
